behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: This failure is most likely in module handoff (file discovery -> filename parsing/sorting -> execution sequencing). A unit test of sorting alone can miss wiring bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess behavior, external protocol contract checks, or cross-process effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the runner against a local test DB with multiple out-of-order `.sql` files in `migrations/`; verify execution/effects occur in ascending prefix order.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: Idempotency depends on interaction between migration execution and `_migrations` state persisted across runs; unit-only tests can miss state handoff bugs. Rejected System: behavior text asks for subsequent-run skip logic, not subprocess/OS semantics or external contract validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute runner twice against the same local DB; assert the second run skips previously recorded versions and does not reapply their SQL effects.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: This is transaction + orchestration behavior across executor, DB transaction handling, and run control. Unit tests can miss rollback/stop interaction. Rejected System: no explicit requirement for subprocess/OS/network contract behavior; local DB integration is sufficient reality signal.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local DB with a migration that partially writes then fails, followed by another migration; verify failed migration effects are absent (rollback) and later migration is not applied.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires concurrency from separate processes, which is real subprocess/coordination behavior not faithfully reproduced by unit or in-process-only integration mocks. Also requires round-trip proof of externally visible side effect (exactly-once application in DB).
behaviors.test_description: Launch two real runner processes simultaneously against the same DB and migration set; after completion, verify each migration’s DB effect and `_migrations` record appears exactly once overall.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: This checks initialization ordering across components (bootstrap metadata table creation -> migration application). Unit tests of individual functions can miss sequencing bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess behavior or external protocol/error-contract verification.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a fresh local DB and migration files; verify `_migrations` is created first and then migration SQL is applied, with ordering confirmed via observed DB state.
